description: Dependency DAG of the Proximity Prize program. Nodes = statements/artifacts/experiments; edges point FROM a dependency TO the statement it supports. gate=all: every 'req' child needed; gate=any: one 'alt' child suffices (plus all 'req'). Statuses: PROVED (on main w/ proof or exact verification), PROVABLE (route pinned, write-up pending), CONDITIONAL (proved under stated hypothesis), CONJECTURE (precise statement, unproved), TARGET (fuzzy, expected to sharpen), WALL (frozen hard core w/ consumer theorem), REFUTED (dead route), TEST (experiment/prediction). Source of truth for statuses: experimental/notes/roadmaps/ (r2 + proof_sketch + wp_detail). REFINEMENT PRINCIPLES (2026-07-02): (1) critical nodes carry exact 'statement' fields (+ attack_surface/falsifier notes) — machine-enforced; (2) new conjectured nodes are admitted ONLY as branches of exhaustive splits, proved equivalences/reductions, or with a named non-ev consumer + truth-confidence note — never as speculative lemma chains presuming a proof shape; (3) every critical conjecture gets a cheap wired falsifier TEST. INTEGRATED UPSTREAM 2026-07-02 (commit 01add412, PR #174 closed-integrated): this DAG is in-tree subordinate roadmap material; towards-prize.md is the source of truth.

nodes.statement: For classes differing in one element, e1(B) - e1(B') = zeta^i - zeta^j has all archimedean conjugates <= 2, so its norm is <= 2^{phi(N')} ~ 2^{N'/2} << p ~ 2^250. Nonzero (thm:upstairs) and of height below p => p cannot divide it => single-swap pairs NEVER collide at prize scale. Unconditional, for every prime in the admissible range.
nodes.notes: the base case of certified distinctness: the swap-metric graph's edges are all collision-free; the open problem is composing this into large all-pairs-distinct families | Subsumed as the s=1 case of graded_collision_radius; kept as the write-up's base case.

nodes.title: The deciding scale is PINNED in an absolute window (n-uniformity of the census)
nodes.statement: At agreement A the quotient stratum on scale N' has forced class ratio l'/N' = j/n, so counts grow strictly as 2^{N' H(j/n)} with H(j/n) bounded below on the corridor (j/n ~ 0.4-0.5 => H >= 0.97). Hence exactly ONE scale's count lands within the staircase-step factor of B*, and it lies in an ABSOLUTE window N' in [~120, ~400] — independent of n and k up to 2^40. The census is n-uniform because every crossing only sees bounded scales.
nodes.notes: the lemma that makes a symbolic census possible at all; also gives UNIQUENESS of the deciding scale (monotone counts, q-factor gaps)

nodes.title: Exact bignum evaluation at bounded scales: expansions unnecessary at candidates
nodes.statement: With N' <= ~400 absolute, the deciding class counts K are exactly computable big integers (binomial-sum sized ~2^400 at worst). At candidate points, EXACT K replaces the leading+second-order expansion entirely: the knife-edge condition sharpens from 'estimate cannot decide' to the exact Diophantine condition B* = floor(q/2^128) in [L, K), where L is the certified lower bound strength. (acl_second_order remains needed only for corridor WIDTH/candidate localization, not for tie decisions.)

nodes.title: The undecided set as an explicit function of certification strength L
nodes.statement: For each (rate, candidate A): undecided rows = {admissible q = 1 mod n : floor(q/2^128) in [L(n,A), K(n,A))} — explicit prime-counting windows. Deliverables: the window list, size estimates, and the dodge complement. As L -> K the windows vanish; the census OUTPUT is the map from certification strength to residual undecided rows.

E1's Row-C data already shows margins of 2^22 and 2^79: comfortably dodged.

nodes.statement: For each admissible row and constant m: exhibit adjacent delta with sup_U |Lambda(U, delta-1)| > eps*|F| >= sup_U |Lambda(U, delta)| (per the official m-quantifier). The determination's list half, previously unpriced — the exact mirror of adjacency_closing.

nodes.title: F at dimension 2: elementary pair bound + twins are tangent-paid
nodes.statement: On a gcd-trivial plane P: (i) domain traces H_x cap P are genuine lines; (ii) DISTINCT-trace pairs meet in one point, so sum of C(mult,2) <= C(n,2), giving #(P cap D_j) <= C(n,2)/C(j,2) for twin-free planes — elementary double counting; (iii) TWINS (coincident traces x,y) force every member through x to pass through y: the sub-pencil shares divisor (X-x)(X-y) and f_gcd_reduction pays it as tangent. F-dim-2 = pair bound + twin reduction, with the twin count T entering as (C(n,2) + (q+1)T)/C(j,2).
nodes.attack_surface: write-up + verifier; TESTABLE PREDICTION against E7's own artifact: the j=5 kernel-sample top planes (primitive max 13 > 12 = C(16,2)/C(5,2)) must contain >= 1 twin pair — count twins in the recorded top planes

nodes.notes: explains the E7 numbers: 38 <= 40 at j=3 (bound TIGHTER than the packet's floor 60); the kernel excess 13 > 12 is the twin signature | CROSS-POLLINATION: #183's 'Align E7 evidence with fixed-dimension theorem' commit followed this node's publication on the #178 branch — the census packet is being aligned to the pair-bound frame. | E10 PREDICTION CONFIRMED (#183): 5/5 sampled j=5 top kernel planes contain twins; ZERO twin-free planes above the simple bound 12. Full j=4 Grassmannian census (25,734,890 planes): primitive max 28 vs simple floor 20 vs pair floor 40, all 9 top primitive planes twin-rich (2^8 profile).

nodes.title: The divisor-depth induction: excess concurrency buys shared-divisor depth, one unit per dimension
nodes.statement: F_r for gcd-trivial r-flats by induction: at each point of multiplicity >= j, either the hyperplanes through it are SPREAD in the flat (local pair-type counting binds) or coincidences abound (twin-type structure) — forcing a shared divisor factor and descent to F_{r-1} of the reduced instance. Each dimension of excess buys one unit of divisor depth, matching Conjecture F's j - dim P threshold shape. Base case r = 2 = f_dim2_skeleton.
nodes.attack_surface: the open quantification: the spread-vs-coincidence dichotomy at dim r (how much coincidence forces a factor); naive slicing degrades by q^{r-2} so the divisor structure MUST compound — high plane dimensions merge into the fiber/petal machinery already mapped (perfiber, imgfib)
nodes.falsifier: toy r = 3 census (extend E7) exceeding the compounding-depth prediction

nodes.title: The frame: trace degeneracies = low-weight words of the flat's dual code P-perp
nodes.statement: For a flat P, a subset S of domain points has dependent traces iff P-perp (dual of the evaluation code) contains a word supported on S. Weight 1 = common root (gcd), weight 2 = twins, weight w = the general obstruction; minimal support => all coordinates nonzero => the CLOSURE property (a member vanishing on all-but-one of S vanishes on all of S). If the dual distance w* > r, ALL r-subsets of traces are in general position.
nodes.notes: unifies gcd-reduction (w=1) and twins (w=2) as rungs of one ladder; the syndrome machinery is already dual-code language

nodes.title: Dually-spread flats: r-wise moment count gives C(n,r)/C(j,r) — poly to LOG dimension
nodes.statement: If w*(P-perp) > r: every r-subset of traces meets in exactly one point, so sum of C(mult, r) <= C(n,r), giving #(P cap D_j) <= C(n,r)/C(j,r) ~ (n/j)^r. HONEST SCOPE: poly(n) only for r = O(log n); in-band deficiencies r ~ n 2^-9 exceed this — high dimension MUST route through descent/structure, not moments.

nodes.title: The descent step: a sparse dual word buys divisor depth faster than it costs dimension
nodes.statement: Given a minimal weight-w dual word on S: (a) members containing S in their roots form a subflat of codim <= w-1 (the relation makes one vanishing condition redundant) and factor as l_S x (degree j-w instance) — DEGREE drops w, DIMENSION drops <= w-1: net one unit of divisor depth gained, exactly F's j - dim P threshold shape; (b) by closure, all other members have <= w-2 roots in S: recurse on H minus S with root budget j-w+2. Single-step accounting elementary; assembly belongs to f_dim_induction.

nodes.title: THE RESIDUE: flats with many sparse dual words are quotient/pullback or tangent structured
nodes.statement: RESTATED per E9 (#201, outcome (b) of the pre-registered table): many-sparse flats are pullback-structured, tangent-structured, or DESCENT-REDUCIBLE (the census found a large support-3-only third class — exactly the sparse-dependence branch that f_sparse_descent_step prices). With the descent ledger included BEFORE the primitive residue, the conjecture is: nothing UNPAID survives all three branches.
nodes.attack_surface: coding-theory structure: codes with many minimal low-weight words are classified in known regimes; the coset-support pattern (supports = unions of coset pairs) is the signature to force; toy-enumerable falsifier at n = 16
nodes.falsifier: a toy flat (n = 16 exhaustive over dims 2-3) with many sparse dual words that is neither pullback- nor tangent-classified

nodes.title: The mca_safe assembly implication, priced: constants and conventions uniform over the family
nodes.statement: The implicit lemma 'counting_frame + fm1 + strip + paid_closure + r2 + ext_lift => mca_safe' holds UNIFORMLY over the admissible family: (i) every exponent (B <= 3, B_F, budget arithmetic) is an absolute constant, not n-drifting; (ii) the stratified sum + first-match dedup compose with endpoint conventions printed once (descriptor-generated); (iii) the composition survives k up to 2^40 scales symbolically (wp3_2). The last unpriced composite implication of the adjacency/payment-completeness class.

nodes.title: Graded radius: collisions require swap distance > d*(N',p); small scales unconditionally FULL
nodes.statement: Pairs at swap distance s have e1-difference height <= 2s, norm <= (2s)^{phi(N')}; if (2s)^{phi(N')} < p the pair is collision-free (nonzero by thm:upstairs, norm below p). At N'=128, p ~ 2^250: d* = 7. COROLLARY: when 2l' < p^{1/phi(N')} (true at N' <= 64 for prize p), ALL pairs are certified — unconditional fullness, the prop:qfloor norm-threshold mechanism restated as certification. Subsumes single_swap_injectivity as the s = 1 case.
nodes.notes: PROVED-IN-FLIGHT: #206 (s=1 rung subsumed; exact norm formula Phi_M(1)^(phi(N)/phi(M)))

Any full-strength certification at in-corridor scales must use p-specific input. (Companion anchor, same PR: at most TWO primes in range can collide even half the rate-1/2 cell pairs — the exceptional set is provably tiny.)

nodes.title: THE RESIDUE: generator-factored difference designs + per-row generator certification
nodes.statement: Construct class families F with |F| > B*/poly whose pairwise e1-differences all factor as (unit of height < p^{1/phi}) x (one of g <= poly generators). Then g per-row valuation checks certify ALL pairs of F simultaneously — constant per-row computation replacing 2^244 pairwise checks. The open problem is the DESIGN: Sidon-flavored difference structure in Z[zeta_N'] compatible with the class-to-e1 map. DECOMPOSED: = cluster_certificates (free cliques + one-check pairing + integer freebies, PROVABLE) + generator_economy (multiplicative difference-designs — the sharpened open core, with the root-difference factorization as its proved germ).

nodes.statement: (a) Diameter-d* class sets are fully certified free cliques (~2^33 at N'=128) by graded_collision_radius. (b) For clusters with center-difference Delta EVERYWHERE-BIG (min conjugate >= spread/(budget-1)): every cross-pair diff = Delta(1 + Delta^{-1} eta) with quotient norm < p, so ONE precomputable norm check on Delta certifies ALL cross-pairs of the two clusters. (c) FREEBIE: integer factors are self-certified (p does not divide integers < p), so integer multiples of certified elements are certified.

nodes.statement: Construct cluster-center configurations of size ~B*/2^33 whose pairwise differences lie in (height-budget units) x the MULTIPLICATIVE semigroup generated by g <= poly base elements — then g norm checks certify the entire family (divisibility distributes over products). PROVED GERM: root differences factor as zeta^b (zeta^k - 1): the full difference set of mu_N' is certified by g = N' bases. The open problem: lift this factorization from single roots to l'-sums. ADMISSIBILITY GUARD: the design's existence proof must not consume fiber counts (circularity with perfiber).

Next attacks: multiplicative template compression or imported abelian difference-set designs. No fiber counts used (guard respected).

nodes.title: Anticode/EKR toolkit for exchange-distance independence — with the honest spectral scoping
nodes.statement: Instantiate Delsarte/Frankl-Wilson anticode bounds on J(n,j): sets of locators pairwise exchange-distance > s have size <= C(n-s, j-s)-type bounds — factors (j/n)^s relative to C(n,j). SCOPING NEGATIVE (included): the Johnson gap n is tiny vs degree ~n^2/4, so single-step expander mixing is near-vacuous, and pure combinatorial distance bounds CANNOT reach q-power (FM) scales — the q-scale input must be algebraic (v8 per-locator cap, exchange residual systems). Prevents doomed naive-EML attempts.

nodes.statement: Iterating the weight-w descents (each drops degree >= w, dimension <= w-1; E9 observed w <= 3 suffices at toy scale) produces a descent tree of TOTAL size poly(n), with the spread/moment base at each leaf. Grounded by E9's census multiplicities. With this, F_r assembles for r = O(log n), and high dimension merges into the fiber machinery.

The support-lattice reformulation (f_support_lattice) is what defuses the naive 2^depth danger.

nodes.statement: E13's 71 non-AG exceptions are ALL one-dimensional full-locator-support syzygy circuits, and the PROVED circuit lemma (row dependencies = exactly two zero-polynomial identities; one-dim full-support dependencies with independent deletions are minimal circuits) makes the branch precise: count/classify minimal full-support circuits per (n, j) and price their slope contribution. The exception taxonomy's third and final observed branch.

nodes.title: Reformulation: descent-tree size = closed-set count of the sparse-support lattice
nodes.statement: The naive descent tree is binary (2^depth danger), but tree nodes correspond to CLOSED SETS of the lattice generated by sparse-word supports (unions/closures = subflats + residual domains), with chain length <= dim P + deg drop. Tree size <= lattice size — the accounting identity that makes polynomial termination plausible at all, and the object the family bounds must count.

nodes.statement: For coordinate-prefix/fiber flats the dual is (shortened) MDS: no words below the Singleton range, so the support lattice is trivial and the descent tree has size 1. The moment count applies immediately. Corollary of f_consumer_scoped branch (a).

nodes.statement: For Hankel-pencil kernel flats, displacement structure (V^T D V, #191) forces sparse-word supports into coset patterns — E7's kernel twins were exactly coset pairs. Then the support lattice embeds in the divisor/coset poset: size poly(n). The one genuinely open family-specific termination bound.

nodes.title: Bridge 1: E_3-large = walk-trapping = non-expanding (elementary spectral bookkeeping)
nodes.statement: E_3(A) = <1_A, (P_A M P_A)^3 1_A> (the cubed restricted one-exchange operator), so E_3 large implies large local spectral radius of M on A, which implies small edge expansion of A in J(n,j) (Courant-Fischer). Connects our energy functional to the expansion language of the literature.

nodes.statement: KMS/DKKMS (proved, 2018-19; the 2-to-2 games engine): non-expanding small sets in Johnson graphs correlate with JUNTAS — sets determined by membership of a bounded core of coordinates. Import note + bridge-ledger row + convention matching (their J(n,j), our exchange graph — same object). The XR inverse's stage-2 engine, now NAMED and off-the-shelf.

nodes.statement: A junta on J(n,j) (membership determined by a bounded core C of domain points) is a union of fixed-core/fixed-hole cells; correlation with a junta transfers to correlation with common-divisor (tangent-type, depth |C|) structure — landing exactly in the paid taxonomy. The k=1 dictator extremals (#191, proved) are the base case.

nodes.statement: RESTRUCTURED: primary route = xr_globalness_from_ledger (the proved tangent ledger bounds link densities post-strip) + xr_small_set_engine (KLLM: global sets expand, losses poly in link parameters not 1/mu) + elementary composition arithmetic. The raw-KMS-constants check remains the cheap first look (the loss-exponent table), but the globalness route is expected to carry the FM-scale regime. nodes.statement: Post-strip alignment sets are GLOBAL in the KLLM sense: their density on every fixed-core link is bounded, because link-concentration = aligned locators sharing the core divisor l_S = exactly the tangent stratum, which the PROVED payment machinery (staircase, common-code-line budget, v8 cap) prices. The paid taxonomy and the junta obstruction are the same object from two fields — our ledger supplies the hypothesis the general theorem cannot assume.

nodes.title: Import: global hypercontractivity (KLLM) — global sets expand, at vanishing measure
nodes.statement: Keevash-Lifshitz-Long-Minzer global hypercontractivity (proved): sets with no density boost on any small link expand almost perfectly, with losses polynomial in the link parameters rather than in 1/mu — the density-robust engine for our FM-scale (mu ~ q^{1-t}) regime, replacing raw KMS constants where they degrade. Import note + bridge-ledger row + statement matching.

nodes.title: Circuits are non-generic: the two-identity syzygy kernel vanishes off a determinantal locus
nodes.statement: The proved circuit lemma (#185) says a dependency = TWO simultaneous zero-polynomial identities. By degree counting, the simultaneous-syzygy kernel is generically zero; full-support minimal circuits exist only on an EXPLICIT determinantal locus of configurations (the two kernels intersecting in exactly dimension 1). Circuits are exceptional by construction, not just empirically.
nodes.notes: CAVEAT flagged: if the identities involve (u,v) (not just locator data), the locus lives in (u,v)-space — same argument, different variables; the E13 packet's exact lemma statement settles which

nodes.title: Schwartz-Zippel on the locus: circuit-carrying configurations are q-power suppressed
nodes.statement: The determinantal locus is cut by explicit determinants of locator-coefficient matrices of bounded degree; Schwartz-Zippel gives density <= deg/q^c per configuration family. Combined with combinatorial support counting: total circuit count <= poly(n) x (configurations / q^c) — the q-suppression that makes the branch FM-compatible.

nodes.title: Pricing: each minimal circuit contributes O(1) slopes via the deficiency-one machinery (direct reuse)
nodes.statement: A minimal circuit is a ONE-DIMENSIONAL kernel with independent deletions — exactly the deficiency-1 situation. The U-ladder's Cramer/eliminant dichotomy (u1/u2, in flight in #199) applies verbatim: each circuit yields at most O(1) unpaid slopes (eliminant branch) or lands in a named paid/structured branch (identically-valid branch). The M5 ladder built for A=384 prices the spread exceptions too — direct machinery reuse across walls.

nodes.title: The XR final assembly: three tools partition the distance scales
nodes.statement: For a fixed post-strip pair, bound |A| by the exhaustive per-scale split: (i) CLOSE pairs (exchange distance <= ledger reach s_L): residual systems suppress at a q-FACTOR per step (#152's mechanism); (ii) FAR-ONLY sets (distance-s independent): Delsarte/anticode bounds at (j/n) per step; (iii) STRUCTURED mass (link-dense): the KMS/globalness route (juntas = tangent-paid). The wall closes iff the three coverages overlap — determined by xr_radius_arithmetic. The dichotomy is exhaustive by construction; the assembly is the wall.

nodes.title: Each residual constraint is worth a q-factor: the ledger's exchange rate over combinatorics
nodes.statement: From the #152 structure: each exchange step's residual constraint is an algebraic equation over F_q, so aligned-pair probability at distance s carries q^{-c(s,t)} suppression (c >= 1 per overdetermined step) — versus the anticode's (j/n) ~ 2^{-1} per step. One algebraic step buys what ~log q ~ 250 combinatorial steps buy. Formalize c(s,t) from the residual-system ranks (t = 2, s = 1 proved; the general shape is the target of exchange_ledger_gen_t).

nodes.title: The coverage table: compute the REQUIRED ledger reach s*(rate) — a number, not a hope
nodes.statement: Pure arithmetic once c(s,t) is parameterized: per rate, tabulate total suppression from (ledger q-powers up to s_L) + (anticode factors beyond) + (KMS-side structured absorption) against the FM target q^{t-1}/n^B; solve for the minimal ledger reach s* that closes the gap. Turns exchange_ledger_gen_t from 'generalize past t = 2' into 'reach s* = <specific number>' — and reveals immediately whether s* is small (soft wall) or grows with t (hard wall).
nodes.notes: the single most information-dense computation available on the XR front: it prices the whole wall
